steps.title: Substitute t = 0 into the function
steps.content:
The question asks for the temperature when the beaker was placed on the table, which is the initial time $t = 0$. Substitute into the given function:
$$g(0) = 294 + (363 - 294)(2.72)^{-0.103(0)}$$

steps.title: Simplify and calculate
steps.content:
The exponent becomes $-0.103(0) = 0$. Since any nonzero number raised to the power 0 equals 1:
$$(2.72)^{0} = 1$$
Therefore:
$$g(0) = 294 + (363 - 294)(1) = 294 + 69 = 363$$
The temperature of the liquid when the beaker was first placed on the table was approximately 363 kelvins.
